codeReview.summary: The fix properly addresses the blocking issues from round 1 review. VAL-A11Y-002 now checks CSS computed styles for focus indicators (outline, box-shadow, border, background-color, transform) instead of just verifying element has focus. VAL-A11Y-004 now implements actual WCAG luminance contrast ratio calculation (4.5:1 for normal text, 3:1 for large text) instead of just checking non-transparent color. The button contrast test correctly identifies a real accessibility issue in the app (#f5f0e8 on #c9a227 = 2.13:1, below 3:1 threshold).

issues.description: The hasVisibleFocusIndicator helper checks background-color change with `bgColor !== 'transparent' && bgColor !== 'rgba(0, 0, 0, 0)'`. However, this could potentially flag non-focus-related background colors if an element already has a background set. The logic is sound for focus detection in most cases, but edge cases where element has permanent background may need monitoring.

Navigation links contrast test (line 656-672) only verifies lowestRatio is finite, not that it passes WCAG AA. The test is less strict than other contrast tests. Consider aligning with the pattern used for other elements.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The WCAG luminance formula implementation in checkContrastMeetsWCAGAA (parseRgb, getLuminance, getContrastRatio) follows the official WCAG 2.1 specification correctly. This is a good reference pattern for future accessibility tests. Consider documenting this pattern in AGENTS.md or creating a shared helper.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: The formula ((L1 + 0.05) / (L2 + 0.05)) with L = 0.2126*R + 0.7152*G + 0.0722*B (after gamma correction) is the correct WCAG 2.1 relative luminance calculation.

summary: The fix addresses both blocking issues from the round 1 review. VAL-A11Y-002 tests now properly verify focus indicators are VISIBLE by checking CSS computed styles (outline, box-shadow, border, background-color, transform). VAL-A11Y-004 tests now properly calculate WCAG AA contrast ratios using the correct luminance formula. The tests now correctly identify accessibility issues - the app's gold button (#c9a227) with light text (#f5f0e8) has only 2.13:1 contrast, below the 3:1 threshold for large text. This is correct test behavior - tests pass only when accessibility standards are met.
